title: Nee Kidaithai Lyrics Chennai 28 2
stanzas:
Nee kidaithai
oru munnai thavam pole
ilam kanni piravaale
idhu undhan anbaale
innaale ponnaale

en ninaivo dhinam
unnai suttrum naane
manam ellam sugamthaane
engum unnai kandene
ennaanen yethaanen

viduthalai aana manam
adimai endravadhenna.
mudivindri povadhenna
munnum pinnum
ennamindri thadumaara

manamellaam thulla thulla
magizhndhodum kaaveri
vizhiyellaam vannam
poosi vilaiyaadiduthe

manamellaam thulla thulla
magizhndhodum kaaveri
maelaeri odaatho
ingum angum engum
pongi paayaadho

en ninaivo dhinam
unnai suttrum naane
manam ellam sugamthaane
engum unnai kandene
ennaanen yethaanen

engae ponaalum
unnudaiya ennam
illaamal ennidathil
sollamale en idhayam
indre pogum

anbudaiya aadhikkame
endrum ennai paadhikkume
aruputhangal saadhikkume
vazhum varai needikkume
neengaathe

Nee kidaithai
oru munnai thavam pole
ilam kanni piravaale
idhu undhan anbaale
innaale ponnaale

en ninaivo dhinam
unnai suttrum naane
manam ellam sugamthaane
engum unnai kandene
ennaanen yethaanen

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Nee Kidaithai'?

The song conveys deep feelings of love and longing, reflecting the emotions of the characters in the movie.

What is the context of this song in the movie 'Chennai 28 II'?

This song plays during a pivotal moment that highlights the romantic relationships among the characters.

Who are the singers of 'Nee Kidaithai'?

The song is beautifully sung by Chinmayi and Haricharan.

Who wrote the lyrics for this song?

The lyrics were penned by Niranjan Bharathi.

When was 'Nee Kidaithai' released?

The song was released on December 9, 2016.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Nee Kidaithai, the characters express their deep affection and yearning for one another, encapsulating the essence of love in a nostalgic manner. The song's soothing melody and heartfelt lyrics evoke a sense of warmth and happiness, making it perfect for romantic occasions. As part of Chennai 28 II, it enhances the emotional depth of the storyline, resonating with anyone who has experienced love. The collaboration between Chinmayi and Haricharan adds a unique charm, making this song a memorable piece in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Nee Kidaithai from Chennai 28 II is a beautiful duet that captures the essence of love and nostalgia. With the enchanting voices of Chinmayi and Haricharan, this song takes listeners on a melodious journey filled with heartfelt emotions. The collaboration between the talented singers and the evocative lyrics makes this track a must-listen for any romantic occasion.
stanzaMeanings: You are like a blessing, like a beautiful girl born from a divine source, this is your love, whether you come or go.
Every day, my thoughts revolve around you, my heart feels at peace, I see you everywhere, what am I, what is happening to me?
What does it mean to have a free heart, to be a slave to love? I can't go on without knowing what lies ahead or behind.
When I create, I feel alive, this is the truth of my existence.
With all my heart, I rejoice, and the beauty of the river flows and plays.
With all my heart, I rejoice, and the river flows everywhere, spreading joy.
Every day, my thoughts revolve around you, my heart feels at peace, I see you everywhere, what am I, what is happening to me?
Wherever I go, without your feelings, my heart will fade away without saying anything.
The intensity of love always guides me, achieving wonders until the end, do not leave me.
My life, my support, my breath, is your eyes.
Every day, my thoughts revolve around you, my heart feels at peace, I see you everywhere, what am I, what is happening to me?
With all my heart, I rejoice, and the river flows everywhere, spreading joy.
